purpose: Fixes queuing commands when HCI_UNREGISTER is set to prevent use-after-free, addressing a bug and improving robustness.
Fixes race conditions and improper vma lock handling during split and unmapping in hugetlb. Ensures correct allocation and freeing of locks to prevent issues like double freeing.
Fixes a resource leak by adding missing iounmap() in remove function.
The commit modifies self-tests to skip RTC tests when the hardware isn't present, preventing unnecessary failures.
The commit addresses sparse warnings by declaring non-global symbols as static in cpu_errata.c.
Allocates space for arrays in struct ctrl_ref to allow dynamic resizing by drivers.
Adds support for specific SoCs and PMICs to the socinfo driver.

The commit aims to reduce the usage of #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL by organizing and grouping related preprocessor directives. It focuses on improving code structure without introducing new functionality or fixing bugs.
Enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware to support future addition of I2C nodes.
Fixes an infinite loop caused by failed HW semaphore acquisition when hardware is removed.
Implements GTP echo response to comply with GTP specification by sending GTP_ECHO_RSP for incoming Echo Requests.
Refactor cpumask functions to use more efficient alternatives.
Removes an obsolete firmware call causing console errors during STA roaming.
Adds board config adl_rt5682 for alc5682 codec without amplifier, connects to SSP0.
The commit modifies checkpatch to stop reporting errors for certain macros used in kernel drivers.
Removes unnecessary VPRINTK() calls and ata_scsi_dump_cdb() as tracepoints provide equivalent information.
Corrects the device tree binding property name from 'reset' to 'resets' and adds missing 'additionalProperties: false'.
The commit silences an array-bounds warning in GCC 12 by adjusting warning levels.
Refactors io_uring by moving fixed file handling to appropriate location and improving context handling.
Updates device tree bindings to split G1 and G2 decoder blocks into separate nodes with independent power-domain control.
